steps.title: Identify what the student assumes
steps.content: The student argues the US should follow Australia's example “with regard to EACH of the four coins,” assuming all four coins present the same financial problem Australia faced—negative seigniorage.

steps.title: Find the evidence contradicting this
steps.content:
Choice B (‘Although issuing one cent and five-cent coins resulted in negative seigniorage in millions of dollars, issuing ten-cent and quarter-dollar coins resulted in positive seigniorage in millions of dollars.’) shows one-cent and five-cent coins did create negative seigniorage, but ten-cent and quarter-dollar coins created positive seigniorage.

Coins generating profits don't fit Australia's situation (which required discontinuation due to losses), so the student's argument fails for two of the four coins.

The other choices either compare values among profitable coins without showing the mix, contain false cost data or rank losses by severity without revealing which coins are profitable.

This confirms B is correct.
